# OpenMDAO medium: file_utils workflow

Wrap concrete `openmdao.utils.file_utils` helpers and inspect a checked-in demo package like a real utility workflow.

## Why This Case Exists

A medium OpenMDAO challenge should go beyond one or two filename helpers and prove that several stable utility routes survive deterministic wrapping.

## Case Shape

- ID: `openmdao_file_utils`
- Family: `openmdao`
- Difficulty: `medium`
- Tier: `gha_subset`
- Style: `package`
- Workflow Slice: `systems`
- Target Kind: `package`
- Upstream Target: `openmdao.utils.file_utils`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/openmdao_file_utils/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

- Minimum generated tools: `5`
- Required generated tools: `fname2mod_name`, `get_module_path`, `get_tempdir`, `is_python_file`, `text2html`

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the case-study lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=openmdao_file_utils
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only openmdao_file_utils
```

## Prepared Inputs

This case does not need rendered setup inputs.

## Fixtures

- `challenges/cases/openmdao_file_utils/fixtures/demo_pkg/__init__.py`
- `challenges/cases/openmdao_file_utils/fixtures/demo_pkg/core.py`

## Workflow

### 1. Confirm the checked-in demo module is a Python file

Tool: `is_python_file`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "file_path": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/demo_pkg/core.py"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['true'].

### 2. Derive the module basename from the checked-in demo module

Tool: `fname2mod_name`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "fname": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/demo_pkg/core.py"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['core'].

### 3. Resolve the import path for the checked-in demo module

Tool: `get_module_path`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "fpath": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/demo_pkg/core.py"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['demo_pkg.core'].

### 4. Return the active temporary directory used by OpenMDAO file utilities

Tool: `get_tempdir`

Expectations: text contains ['tmp'].

### 5. Render a tiny HTML snippet through OpenMDAO utilities

Tool: `text2html`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "text": "OpenMDAO utilities",
  "title": "Demo"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['<!DOCTYPE html>', '<html'].

## What This Case Proves

- Installed engineering utility modules can be scaffolded deterministically in one shot without narrowing to a single function.
- Simple path- and module-oriented helpers survive the generated wrapper path cleanly.
- The generated facade can inspect real checked-in files and render simple HTML output without hand-written glue code.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: start with a stable utility function from a large engineering package.
# This is the cleanest "hello world" for one-shot package ingestion against OpenMDAO.
id = "openmdao_file_utils"
title = "OpenMDAO medium: file_utils workflow"
tier = "gha_subset"
style = "package"
slice = "systems"
family = "openmdao"
difficulty = "medium"

[example]
summary = "Wrap concrete `openmdao.utils.file_utils` helpers and inspect a checked-in demo package like a real utility workflow."
motivation = "A medium OpenMDAO challenge should go beyond one or two filename helpers and prove that several stable utility routes survive deterministic wrapping."
proves = [
  "Installed engineering utility modules can be scaffolded deterministically in one shot without narrowing to a single function.",
  "Simple path- and module-oriented helpers survive the generated wrapper path cleanly.",
  "The generated facade can inspect real checked-in files and render simple HTML output without hand-written glue code.",
]

[target]
kind = "package"
value = "openmdao.utils.file_utils"

[probe]
imports = ["openmdao.utils.file_utils"]

[scaffold]
kind = "package"
symbol_include_patterns = ["^fname2mod_name$", "^get_module_path$", "^get_tempdir$", "^is_python_file$", "^text2html$"]

[ingestion]
min_generated_tools = 5
required_tools = ["fname2mod_name", "get_module_path", "get_tempdir", "is_python_file", "text2html"]

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Confirm the checked-in demo module is a Python file"
tool = "is_python_file"
arguments = { file_path = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/demo_pkg/core.py" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["true"]

[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Derive the module basename from the checked-in demo module"
tool = "fname2mod_name"
arguments = { fname = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/demo_pkg/core.py" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["core"]

[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Resolve the import path for the checked-in demo module"
tool = "get_module_path"
arguments = { fpath = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/demo_pkg/core.py" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["demo_pkg.core"]

[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Return the active temporary directory used by OpenMDAO file utilities"
tool = "get_tempdir"
arguments = {}

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["tmp"]

[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Render a tiny HTML snippet through OpenMDAO utilities"
tool = "text2html"
arguments = { text = "OpenMDAO utilities", title = "Demo" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["<!DOCTYPE html>", "<html"]
```
